artifacts.content:
Hi Mami,

I’m in the library waiting for the late bus so I figured I’d write instead of texting a million times. My stomach has actually been a lot better lately, I think because we’ve been doing more grilled chicken, rice, and plain beans like Dr. Alvarez at El Rio told us after that food poisoning from the carne asada taco at the El Gallo Loco truck by Desert View on April 19. I still kind of miss street tacos, but I don’t want a repeat of that night or him reminding us again to stay away from greasy meats and street tacos at least through October.

I also learned my lesson from last year, when I threw up in the girls’ bathroom at DVHS on September 6, 2023 after eating way too many extra‑spicy Takis and that Flamin’ Hot Cheetos burrito. It was so embarrassing when Ms. Ramirez had to call you, and then I had diarrhea all night at home. So now when people are buying hot chips at lunch, I just take a couple and stop.

Can we get more bananas and saltines again? During finals in May 2024 when I was super stressed and barely sleeping, Ms. Hernandez told me to keep bland snacks in my backpack instead of hot chips and energy drinks from the Circle K on S 6th Ave, and it really helped with the morning nausea.

Also, don’t forget Sofia’s math homework packet is due tomorrow, and Diego needs his water bottle washed. I’ll start the rice when I get home around 4:30.

Love you,
Isabella

Food / stomach reminders – Isabella

1. Spicy stuff
- Remember what happened on 9/6/23 at Desert View: extra‑spicy Takis + Flamin’ Hot Cheetos burrito = puking in the girls’ bathroom, Ms. Ramirez calling Mami, and then diarrhea all night at home. Not worth it.
- Abuela’s visit December 2023: three nights in a row of her super‑spicy chile colorado and leftover tamales at the house (2847 S Camino Sierra Vista) → horrible stomach flare. On December 27 Mami called the Banner Health nurse line and they said it sounded like acid reflux and to avoid very spicy red chiles for a while and keep antacids (Tums) at home.

2. Greasy meats
- April 19, 2024: food poisoning from carne asada taco at El Gallo Loco truck by DVHS. Dr. Alvarez at El Rio Health Center on Congress told us to avoid street tacos and really greasy meats from April–October 2024. Stick to grilled chicken, rice, and plain beans at cookouts.
- May 12, 2024: Reid Park Mother’s Day carne asada. Ate THREE big plates of greasy carne asada, chorizo, chips with queso. Tía Lucía had to drive me to CVS MinuteClinic on E 22nd. Nurse practitioner said probably gastritis from too much fatty food, cut back on greasy meats/chips for at least a month and use OTC antacids if I overdo it.

3. Stress days
- Finals week May 2024: morning nausea from no sleep + energy drinks + hot chips. Ms. Hernandez said keep saltines/bananas in backpack instead of Circle K junk.

Basic rule: bland > spicy/greasy when tired, stressed, or at family parties.

Event: Family cookout at Reid Park (watch food!)
Date: Sunday, May 12, 2024
Time: 11:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.
Location: Reid Park ramada (Tía Lucía reserved it)

Notes:
- Mother’s Day celebration with whole Rodriguez/Torres crew. Abuela maybe coming up from Nogales if she can get a ride.
- BRING: big water bottle (Tucson heat), sunscreen, hoodie for later, soccer ball for Diego and the cousins, Uno cards for Sofia.
- FOOD PLAN (IMPORTANT):
  • Skip piling on greasy carne asada and chorizo like last time. Remember how I went back for three big plates plus chips with queso and ended up super bloated and in pain.
  • If I start to feel that heavy burning feeling again, STOP eating and drink water. Ask for salad, fruit, tortillas with grilled chicken instead.
  • If it gets bad, tell Mami or Tía Lucía early. Last time on 5/12/24 she had to drive me from Reid Park to the CVS MinuteClinic on E 22nd St, and the nurse practitioner said it was probably gastritis from too much fatty food and to cut back on greasy meats and chips for at least a month.
- Pack a small bag with: Tums or other antacid, tissues (allergies), contact lens case + glasses.
- Try to get homework (English reading + math problems) mostly done Saturday so I’m not stressing Sunday night.

Reminder: don’t forget phone charger so I can text Javier and take pics.

Hola Abuela,

Te escribo un ratito antes de empezar la tarea. I was thinking about Christmas last year when you stayed with us in December 2023. I still remember how good your chile colorado and the tamales were, but also how my stomach freaked out after we ate them three nights in a row at the house on S Camino Sierra Vista. Poor Mami ended up calling the Banner Health nurse line on December 27 because I had that burning feeling in my chest and stomach, and the nurse said it sounded like acid reflux and to avoid super spicy red chiles for a while and keep Tums in the kitchen.

Don’t worry, I still love your cooking, I just have to be a little more careful. Same with tacos and greasy meat. After that carne asada taco from the El Gallo Loco food truck by Desert View on April 19, 2024 gave me food poisoning, Dr. Alvarez at El Rio Health Center on Congress told us to stay away from street tacos and really greasy meats from April to October. So at cookouts I’ve been doing grilled chicken, rice, and plain beans instead of a mountain of carne asada and chorizo.

It’s kind of funny because when I was younger I could eat anything and be fine. Now between that and the time I got sick at school from extra‑spicy Takis, I’m learning my lesson. I still want to help you make tamales at Christmas, maybe we can do a milder batch just for me and Sofia.

Tell everyone in Nogales I say hi. I’ll try to call you Sunday after we get back from church.

Te quiero mucho,
Isabella

[Group chat: “DVHS Besties” – Isabella, Ana, Karla]

2024-05-20 7:12 AM – Isabella: 
Ugh my stomach is already weird and it’s not even 1st period yet.

7:13 AM – Ana:
Girl did you eat breakfast?

7:14 AM – Isabella:
Barely. During finals week last May 2024 I was so stressed and barely sleeping that I kept skipping breakfast and living on energy drinks + hot chips from the Circle K on S 6th Ave. Ended up with morning nausea every day. Ms. Hernandez made me promise to keep saltines and bananas in my backpack instead, so I had like 4 crackers lol.

7:16 AM – Karla:
Circle K hot chips are a trap 😂

7:17 AM – Isabella:
For real. Remember when I legit puked in the Desert View girls’ bathroom on 9/6/23 after the extra‑spicy Takis and that Flamin’ Hot Cheetos burrito?? Ms. Ramirez had to call my mom and I had diarrhea all night. Never again.

7:19 AM – Ana:
Omg yes that was horrible. At least now you know your limit.

7:20 AM – Isabella:
Yeah and then there was the El Gallo Loco taco incident by school on 4/19/24 🙃 Dr. Alvarez at El Rio Health Center on Congress told me and my mom to chill on street tacos and greasy meats till like October. So today I’m being good: water bottle, crackers, banana, no hot Cheetos.

7:22 AM – Karla:
Proud of you 😂 also don’t die before the history test.

7:23 AM – Isabella:
Trying!! See you in 2nd period.

Weekly plan – school, family, health

Monday
- Pack: contact lens case + glasses, tissues (allergies), reusable water bottle (Tucson heat headaches), saltines + banana.
- Lunch: avoid super spicy chips. Remember 9/6/23 DVHS girls’ bathroom incident (Takis + Flamin’ Hot Cheetos burrito → vomiting, Ms. Ramirez calling Mami, diarrhea all night). Just share a few chips if friends have them.

Tuesday
- After school, help Sofia with math and Diego with reading.
- Check if we’re low on antacids at home. Since the Banner Health nurse line call on 12/27/23 (after Abuela’s super‑spicy chile colorado and tamales three nights in a row), Mami wants to always have Tums in the kitchen.

Wednesday
- Leadership club meeting. Bring water/snack so I don’t end up at Circle K on S 6th Ave buying energy drinks and hot chips. During May 2024 finals that combo + no sleep = morning nausea every day until Ms. Hernandez told me to keep bland snacks in my backpack.

Thursday
- If there’s a cookout invite coming up (like at Reid Park with Tía Lucía), plan to stick to grilled chicken, rice, and plain beans. After the 4/19/24 El Gallo Loco food poisoning, Dr. Alvarez at El Rio Health Center said no street tacos/greasy meats April–October.

Friday
- Check grades in English/history, aim to finish homework before the weekend family stuff.

General: bland food when stressed, go easy on spicy red chile and greasy meats, drink water, breathe.
